primary_agent.label: Primary agent
primary_agent.role: Receives the work order first, self-reviews it, and either resolves within scope or reports upward based on routing policy.

persona_operator_agent.label: Persona or operator agent
persona_operator_agent.role: Reviews voice, content quality, topic choice, and operator behavior.

specialized_engineering_agent.label: Specialized engineering agent
specialized_engineering_agent.role: Edits code, tests, schemas, documentation, deployment scripts, or rollback tooling.

stronger_model.label: Stronger model
stronger_model.role: Handles high-complexity repair or design work after the user chooses to escalate.

human_owner.label: Human owner
human_owner.role: Approves high-risk, durable, public, credential, or production-impacting actions.

work_orders.title: Auto-L3 non-skill promotion from local distillation sources
work_orders.summary: Broad Auto-L3 would promote case, memory and policy lessons from local distillation sources into skills. Minimal-positive mode blocked the export, so this is a local design/regression risk, not a live production incident. Keep regression coverage and repair-ticket wording specific enough that future agents know which route owns each lesson.

source_refs.note: Keep this as case, not L3 skill export.
Keep this as policy, not L3 skill export.
Keep this as memory, not L3 skill export.

delivery.reason: The routing policy sends the work order to the primary agent first so it can self-review, capture experience, and report upward only if needed.

suggested_executor.label: Specialized engineering agent
suggested_executor.reason: The ticket includes code/schema/test scope and concrete acceptance checks.

reasons: The ticket has enough scope to justify a work order.
The ticket represents a real repair candidate.
The ticket includes reproduction commands, acceptance criteria, and edit scope.
Error discovery cost is bounded by local reproduction and acceptance checks.

acceptance_criteria: every exported skill has route_target == skill

escalation.recommended_when: Escalate if the primary agent cannot finish the repair safely or the diff spans multiple subsystems.

work_orders.user_prompt: I received a work order: Auto-L3 non-skill promotion from local distillation sources. Summary: Broad Auto-L3 would promote case, memory and policy lessons from local distillation sources into skills. Minimal-positive mode blocked the export, so this is a local design/regression risk, not a live production incident. Keep regression coverage and repair-ticket wording specific enough that future agents know which route owns each lesson. Suggested executor: Specialized engineering agent. Risk level: high (local design/regression risk; minimal-positive mode already blocked the bad export). Do you want me to handle it, keep it pending, or hand it to a stronger model?

model_handoff.reason: The work order is high risk, broad, or complex enough that a stronger model should be offered before execution. Durable or public effects remain blocked.

warnings: Work orders are routing packets, not automatic execution.
The primary agent may self-review first, but durable or public effects still stay blocked from silent execution.
Every suggested executor can be overridden by the user.
